name: World Capitals
description: Type the capital city of all 197 countries: every UN member and observer state, plus Kosovo and Taiwan. Covers multi-capital countries and recent renames like Astana and Gitega.

fields.front: What is the capital of France?
fields.extra: Also the largest city in France, on the Seine.

fields.front: What is the capital of Spain?
fields.extra: Not Barcelona.

fields.front: What is the capital of Italy?
fields.extra: Surrounds Vatican City, which is a separate country.

fields.front: What is the capital of Germany?
fields.extra: Capital again since reunification in 1990; Bonn was West Germany's capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of the United Kingdom?
fields.extra: Capital of both the UK and England.

fields.front: What is the capital of Japan?
fields.extra: Not Kyoto, the historical capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of the United States?
fields.extra: Washington, D.C. (District of Columbia). Not New York City.

fields.front: What is the capital of Canada?
fields.extra: Not Toronto, Montreal, or Vancouver.

fields.front: What is the capital of Brazil?
fields.extra: Brasília, a purpose-built capital inaugurated in 1960. Not Rio de Janeiro or Sao Paulo.

fields.front: What is the capital of Australia?
fields.extra: Not Sydney or Melbourne; Canberra was purpose-built as a compromise between the two.

fields.front: What is the capital of China?
fields.extra: Means northern capital. Not Shanghai.

fields.front: What is the capital of India?
fields.extra: New Delhi is the official capital, a district within the larger city of Delhi.

fields.front: What is the capital of Russia?
fields.extra: Not Saint Petersburg, the imperial capital until 1918.

fields.front: What is the capital of Mexico?
fields.extra: Spanish: Ciudad de México (CDMX).

fields.front: What is the capital of Egypt?
fields.extra: Largest city in the Arab world. A New Administrative Capital is being built east of Cairo.

fields.front: What is the capital of Turkey?
fields.extra: Not Istanbul, the largest city and former Ottoman capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Greece?
fields.extra: Named for the goddess Athena.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Netherlands?
fields.extra: Constitutional capital. The government, parliament, and king sit in The Hague.

fields.front: What is the capital of Portugal?
fields.extra: Portuguese: Lisboa.

fields.front: What is the capital of Switzerland?
fields.extra: Not Zurich or Geneva. Officially the federal city rather than the capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Argentina?
fields.extra: Autonomous city on the Rio de la Plata.

fields.front: What is the capital of South Korea?
fields.extra: One of the largest metropolitan areas in the world.

fields.front: What is the capital of South Africa?
fields.extra: Executive capital. Cape Town is the legislative capital (parliament) and Bloemfontein the judicial capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of New Zealand?
fields.extra: Not Auckland, the largest city. Often cited as the world's southernmost capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Albania?
fields.extra: Albanian: Tiranë.

fields.front: What is the capital of Andorra?
fields.extra: Highest capital in Europe, in the Pyrenees.

fields.front: What is the capital of Austria?
fields.extra: German: Wien.

fields.front: What is the capital of Belarus?
fields.extra: On the Svislach River.

fields.front: What is the capital of Belgium?
fields.extra: Also the de facto capital of the European Union.

fields.front: What is the capital of Bosnia and Herzegovina?
fields.extra: Hosted the 1984 Winter Olympics.

fields.front: What is the capital of Bulgaria?
fields.extra: One of Europe's oldest cities, at the foot of Vitosha mountain.

fields.front: What is the capital of Croatia?
fields.extra: Not Split or Dubrovnik.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Czech Republic (Czechia)?
fields.extra: Czech: Praha.

fields.front: What is the capital of Denmark?
fields.extra: Danish: København.

fields.front: What is the capital of Estonia?
fields.extra: Double l and double n.

fields.front: What is the capital of Finland?
fields.extra: On the Gulf of Finland, across from Tallinn.

fields.front: What is the capital of Hungary?
fields.extra: Buda and Pest, merged across the Danube in 1873. Not to be confused with Bucharest.

fields.front: What is the capital of Iceland?
fields.extra: Reykjavík. Northernmost capital of a sovereign state.

fields.front: What is the capital of Ireland?
fields.extra: Irish: Baile Átha Cliath.

fields.front: What is the capital of Kosovo?
fields.extra: Also spelled Prishtina (Albanian) or Priština (Serbian).

fields.front: What is the capital of Latvia?
fields.extra: Largest city of the three Baltic states.

fields.front: What is the capital of Liechtenstein?
fields.extra: On the Rhine, between Switzerland and Austria.

fields.front: What is the capital of Lithuania?
fields.extra: Not Kaunas, the interwar capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Luxembourg?
fields.extra: Luxembourg City; the capital shares the country's name.

fields.front: What is the capital of Malta?
fields.extra: Double l and double t. One of the smallest capitals in the EU.

fields.front: What is the capital of Moldova?
fields.extra: Chișinău.

fields.front: What is the capital of Monaco?
fields.extra: City-state; Monaco-Ville is the old town district.

fields.front: What is the capital of Montenegro?
fields.extra: Called Titograd in Yugoslav times.

fields.front: What is the capital of North Macedonia?
fields.extra: Birthplace of Mother Teresa.

fields.front: What is the capital of Norway?
fields.extra: Called Christiania until 1925.

fields.front: What is the capital of Poland?
fields.extra: Polish: Warszawa. Not Krakow, the former royal capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Romania?
fields.extra: Romanian: București. Not to be confused with Budapest.

fields.front: What is the capital of San Marino?
fields.extra: The City of San Marino shares the country's name.

fields.front: What is the capital of Serbia?
fields.extra: Serbian: Beograd, meaning white city.

fields.front: What is the capital of Slovakia?
fields.extra: On the Danube, less than an hour from Vienna.

fields.front: What is the capital of Slovenia?
fields.extra: The j appears twice: Lju-blja-na.

fields.front: What is the capital of Sweden?
fields.extra: Spread across 14 islands.

fields.front: What is the capital of Ukraine?
fields.extra: Preferred romanization; Kiev is the older Russian-derived spelling.

fields.front: What is the capital of Vatican City?
fields.extra: A city-state; the country and its capital are one and the same. Smallest country in the world.

fields.front: What is the capital of Afghanistan?
fields.extra: In a valley of the Hindu Kush.

fields.front: What is the capital of Armenia?
fields.extra: One of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities.

fields.front: What is the capital of Azerbaijan?
fields.extra: Lowest-lying national capital, below sea level on the Caspian.

fields.front: What is the capital of Bahrain?
fields.extra: Not to be confused with Muscat or Doha, its Gulf neighbors.

fields.front: What is the capital of Bangladesh?
fields.extra: Formerly spelled Dacca.

fields.front: What is the capital of Bhutan?
fields.extra: One of the few capitals without traffic lights.

fields.front: What is the capital of Brunei?
fields.extra: Formerly known as Brunei Town.

fields.front: What is the capital of Cambodia?
fields.extra: At the confluence of the Mekong and Tonle Sap rivers.

fields.front: What is the capital of Cyprus?
fields.extra: The world's last divided capital, split by the UN buffer zone.

fields.front: What is the capital of Georgia?
fields.extra: Formerly known as Tiflis.

fields.front: What is the capital of Indonesia?
fields.extra: A new capital, Nusantara, is under construction on Borneo, but Jakarta remains the capital until the transfer is formalized.

fields.front: What is the capital of Iran?
fields.extra: At the foot of the Alborz mountains.

fields.front: What is the capital of Iraq?
fields.extra: On the Tigris.

fields.front: What is the capital of Israel?
fields.extra: Proclaimed capital; international recognition is divided and most embassies are in Tel Aviv.

fields.front: What is the capital of Jordan?
fields.extra: Known as Philadelphia in Roman times.

fields.front: What is the capital of Kazakhstan?
fields.extra: Named Nur-Sultan from 2019 to 2022, then renamed back to Astana. Not Almaty, the former capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Kuwait?
fields.extra: The capital shares the country's name.

fields.front: What is the capital of Kyrgyzstan?
fields.extra: Called Frunze in Soviet times.

fields.front: What is the capital of Laos?
fields.extra: On the Mekong, across from Thailand.

fields.front: What is the capital of Lebanon?
fields.extra: Once called the Paris of the Middle East.

fields.front: What is the capital of Malaysia?
fields.extra: Putrajaya is the federal administrative centre, but Kuala Lumpur remains the capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Maldives?
fields.extra: Malé. One of the most densely populated islands on earth.

fields.front: What is the capital of Mongolia?
fields.extra: Also written Ulan Bator. The coldest national capital in the world.

fields.front: What is the capital of Myanmar (Burma)?
fields.extra: Also written Nay Pyi Taw. Purpose-built capital since 2006; not Yangon (Rangoon), the former capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Nepal?
fields.extra: Gateway to the Himalayas.

fields.front: What is the capital of North Korea?
fields.extra: On the Taedong River.

fields.front: What is the capital of Oman?
fields.extra: Not to be confused with Manama, Bahrain's capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Pakistan?
fields.extra: Purpose-built in the 1960s. Not Karachi, the largest city and first capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Palestine?
fields.extra: Administrative center of the Palestinian Authority; East Jerusalem is the proclaimed capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Philippines?
fields.extra: Part of Metro Manila; Quezon City was the capital from 1948 to 1976.

fields.front: What is the capital of Qatar?
fields.extra: Hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup final.

fields.front: What is the capital of Saudi Arabia?
fields.extra: Not Mecca or Jeddah.

fields.front: What is the capital of Singapore?
fields.extra: A city-state; the country and its capital are the same.

fields.front: What is the capital of Sri Lanka?
fields.extra: The official legislative capital, a suburb of Colombo. Colombo is the commercial capital and the answer often given, but it is not the official one.

fields.front: What is the capital of Syria?
fields.extra: Often cited as the oldest continuously inhabited capital in the world.

fields.front: What is the capital of Taiwan?
fields.extra: Home of the Taipei 101 tower.

fields.front: What is the capital of Tajikistan?
fields.extra: Means Monday in Tajik, after its market day.

fields.front: What is the capital of Thailand?
fields.extra: Thai: Krung Thep.

fields.front: What is the capital of Timor-Leste (East Timor)?
fields.extra: On the north coast of Timor island.

fields.front: What is the capital of Turkmenistan?
fields.extra: Known for its white marble buildings.

fields.front: What is the capital of the United Arab Emirates?
fields.extra: Not Dubai, the largest city.

fields.front: What is the capital of Uzbekistan?
fields.extra: Largest city in Central Asia.

fields.front: What is the capital of Vietnam?
fields.extra: Not Ho Chi Minh City (Saigon), the largest city.

fields.front: What is the capital of Yemen?
fields.extra: Sana'a. Aden serves as the seat of the internationally recognized government during the civil war.

fields.front: What is the capital of Algeria?
fields.extra: French: Alger. The capital's name echoes the country's.

fields.front: What is the capital of Angola?
fields.extra: Not to be confused with Lusaka, Zambia's capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Benin?
fields.extra: Official capital; the government largely operates from Cotonou.

fields.front: What is the capital of Botswana?
fields.extra: Pronounced ha-bo-ro-neh.

fields.front: What is the capital of Burkina Faso?
fields.extra: Often shortened to Ouaga.

fields.front: What is the capital of Burundi?
fields.extra: Capital since 2019, moved from Bujumbura, which remains the economic capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Cabo Verde (Cape Verde)?
fields.extra: Means beach in Portuguese.

fields.front: What is the capital of Cameroon?
fields.extra: Yaoundé. Not Douala, the largest city.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Central African Republic?
fields.extra: On the Ubangi River.

fields.front: What is the capital of Chad?
fields.extra: Formerly Fort-Lamy.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Comoros?
fields.extra: On the island of Grande Comore.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Republic of the Congo?
fields.extra: Faces Kinshasa across the Congo River, the world's closest pair of capitals.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Democratic Republic of the Congo?
fields.extra: Formerly Leopoldville. Faces Brazzaville across the Congo River.

fields.front: What is the capital of Djibouti?
fields.extra: Djibouti City; the capital shares the country's name.

fields.front: What is the capital of Equatorial Guinea?
fields.extra: On the island of Bioko, not the mainland. A new capital, Ciudad de la Paz, is under construction.

fields.front: What is the capital of Eritrea?
fields.extra: Known for its Italian modernist architecture.

fields.front: What is the capital of Eswatini (formerly Swaziland)?
fields.extra: Administrative capital; Lobamba is the royal and legislative capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Ethiopia?
fields.extra: Headquarters of the African Union. Means new flower in Amharic.

fields.front: What is the capital of Gabon?
fields.extra: Means free town in French, founded by freed slaves.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Gambia?
fields.extra: Formerly Bathurst, on an island at the mouth of the Gambia River.

fields.front: What is the capital of Ghana?
fields.extra: On the Gulf of Guinea.

fields.front: What is the capital of Guinea?
fields.extra: Not to be confused with Bissau (Guinea-Bissau) or Malabo (Equatorial Guinea).

fields.front: What is the capital of Guinea-Bissau?
fields.extra: The capital appears in the country's name.

fields.front: What is the capital of Ivory Coast (Côte d'Ivoire)?
fields.extra: Official capital since 1983. Abidjan remains the economic hub and de facto seat of government.

fields.front: What is the capital of Kenya?
fields.extra: Home to a national park within city limits.

fields.front: What is the capital of Lesotho?
fields.extra: On the border with South Africa.

fields.front: What is the capital of Liberia?
fields.extra: Named after US President James Monroe.

fields.front: What is the capital of Libya?
fields.extra: Not to be confused with Tripoli, Lebanon.

fields.front: What is the capital of Madagascar?
fields.extra: Often shortened to Tana.

fields.front: What is the capital of Malawi?
fields.extra: Not Blantyre, the commercial center.

fields.front: What is the capital of Mali?
fields.extra: On the Niger River. Not Timbuktu.

fields.front: What is the capital of Mauritania?
fields.extra: One of the largest cities in the Sahara.

fields.front: What is the capital of Mauritius?
fields.extra: Named after King Louis XV of France.

fields.front: What is the capital of Morocco?
fields.extra: Not Casablanca, the largest city, and not Marrakesh.

fields.front: What is the capital of Mozambique?
fields.extra: Called Lourenço Marques until 1976.

fields.front: What is the capital of Namibia?
fields.extra: German colonial name meaning windy corner.

fields.front: What is the capital of Niger?
fields.extra: On the Niger River. Not to be confused with Abuja, Nigeria's capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Nigeria?
fields.extra: Purpose-built capital since 1991. Not Lagos, the largest city and former capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Rwanda?
fields.extra: Known as one of the cleanest cities in Africa.

fields.front: What is the capital of Sao Tome and Principe?
fields.extra: São Tomé. The capital shares the main island's name.

fields.front: What is the capital of Senegal?
fields.extra: Westernmost capital on the African mainland.

fields.front: What is the capital of Seychelles?
fields.extra: One of the smallest capitals in the world, on the island of Mahe.

fields.front: What is the capital of Sierra Leone?
fields.extra: Founded as a settlement for freed slaves.

fields.front: What is the capital of Somalia?
fields.extra: On the Indian Ocean coast.

fields.front: What is the capital of South Sudan?
fields.extra: On the White Nile. The world's newest capital of a UN member state.

fields.front: What is the capital of Sudan?
fields.extra: At the confluence of the Blue and White Nile.

fields.front: What is the capital of Tanzania?
fields.extra: Official capital since 1996. Not Dar es Salaam, the largest city and former capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Togo?
fields.extra: Lomé.

fields.front: What is the capital of Tunisia?
fields.extra: Near the ruins of ancient Carthage.

fields.front: What is the capital of Uganda?
fields.extra: On the shores of Lake Victoria.

fields.front: What is the capital of Zambia?
fields.extra: Not to be confused with Luanda, Angola's capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Zimbabwe?
fields.extra: Called Salisbury until 1982.

fields.front: What is the capital of Antigua and Barbuda?
fields.extra: Usually written St. John's. Not to be confused with St. John's, Newfoundland, or San Juan, Puerto Rico.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Bahamas?
fields.extra: On New Providence island.

fields.front: What is the capital of Barbados?
fields.extra: A UNESCO World Heritage site.

fields.front: What is the capital of Belize?
fields.extra: Purpose-built inland after Hurricane Hattie hit Belize City in 1961. Not Belize City.

fields.front: What is the capital of Bolivia?
fields.extra: Seat of government and the world's highest administrative capital. Sucre is the constitutional capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Chile?
fields.extra: Valparaiso hosts the national congress.

fields.front: What is the capital of Colombia?
fields.extra: Bogotá. High in the Andes at about 2,640 m.

fields.front: What is the capital of Costa Rica?
fields.extra: San José. Not to be confused with San Jose, California, or San Juan, Puerto Rico.

fields.front: What is the capital of Cuba?
fields.extra: Spanish: La Habana.

fields.front: What is the capital of Dominica?
fields.extra: Dominica is the island nation, distinct from the Dominican Republic.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Dominican Republic?
fields.extra: Oldest continuously inhabited European settlement in the Americas.

fields.front: What is the capital of Ecuador?
fields.extra: Second-highest capital in the world, after La Paz. Not Guayaquil, the largest city.

fields.front: What is the capital of El Salvador?
fields.extra: The capital's name echoes the country's.

fields.front: What is the capital of Grenada?
fields.extra: Usually written St. George's.

fields.front: What is the capital of Guatemala?
fields.extra: The capital shares the country's name.

fields.front: What is the capital of Guyana?
fields.extra: Largely below sea level, protected by a seawall.

fields.front: What is the capital of Haiti?
fields.extra: Hyphenated: Port-au-Prince.

fields.front: What is the capital of Honduras?
fields.extra: Often shortened to Tegus.

fields.front: What is the capital of Jamaica?
fields.extra: Not to be confused with Kingstown, capital of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines.

fields.front: What is the capital of Nicaragua?
fields.extra: On the shore of Lake Managua.

fields.front: What is the capital of Panama?
fields.extra: At the Pacific entrance of the Panama Canal.

fields.front: What is the capital of Paraguay?
fields.extra: Asunción. One of the oldest cities in South America.

fields.front: What is the capital of Peru?
fields.extra: Not Cusco, the old Inca capital.

fields.front: What is the capital of Saint Kitts and Nevis?
fields.extra: One word. Not to be confused with Basse-Terre, Guadeloupe.

fields.front: What is the capital of Saint Lucia?
fields.extra: Named after a French minister of the navy.

fields.front: What is the capital of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines?
fields.extra: With a w. Not Kingston, Jamaica.

fields.front: What is the capital of Suriname?
fields.extra: A UNESCO-listed Dutch colonial old town.

fields.front: What is the capital of Trinidad and Tobago?
fields.extra: Sometimes written Port-of-Spain.

fields.front: What is the capital of Uruguay?
fields.extra: Southernmost capital in the Americas.

fields.front: What is the capital of Venezuela?
fields.extra: In a valley near the Caribbean coast.

fields.front: What is the capital of Fiji?
fields.extra: On the island of Viti Levu.

fields.front: What is the capital of Kiribati?
fields.extra: Formally South Tarawa, on Tarawa atoll.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Marshall Islands?
fields.extra: An atoll as well as the capital city.

fields.front: What is the capital of Micronesia?
fields.extra: On the island of Pohnpei.

fields.front: What is the capital of Nauru?
fields.extra: Nauru has no official capital; Yaren is the de facto seat of government.

fields.front: What is the capital of Palau?
fields.extra: Least populous capital in the world; replaced Koror in 2006.

fields.front: What is the capital of Papua New Guinea?
fields.extra: On the Gulf of Papua.

fields.front: What is the capital of Samoa?
fields.extra: On the island of Upolu.

fields.front: What is the capital of the Solomon Islands?
fields.extra: On Guadalcanal island.

fields.front: What is the capital of Tonga?
fields.extra: The apostrophe stands for the Tongan okina and is often omitted (Nukualofa).

fields.front: What is the capital of Tuvalu?
fields.extra: An atoll; Vaiaku village hosts the government offices.

fields.front: What is the capital of Vanuatu?
fields.extra: Sometimes written Port-Vila, on the island of Efate.
